ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 does not have a dedicated code for Kachumber Salad; A00GJ (mixed vegetable salad) is the closest defensible category anchor.

serving.natural_language_portion: A standard serving of Kachumber Salad is approximately 150 g, equivalent to one medium-sized side salad bowl typically served alongside a main Indian meal.

nutrient_source.title: Nutritive Value of Indian Foods (ICMR-NIN)
nutrient_source.organization: ICMR-NIN (Indian Council of Medical Research – National Institute of Nutrition)
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Kachumber Salad is a composite raw vegetable dish. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN published data for cucumber, tomato, onion, and lemon juice in proportions consistent with a standard Kachumber recipe. Sodium value reflects light salting typical of the dish. Cholesterol value of 26 mg/100g from the input record is flagged as anomalous for a plant-based raw salad (expected 0 mg); it has been corrected to 0.0 mg as no animal-derived ingredients are present. All other values are consistent with ICMR-NIN component data.

gi_source.title: International Tables of Glycemic Index and Glycemic Load Values: 2021
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al., American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for Kachumber Salad. GI of 15 is estimated by analogy with raw mixed vegetable salads (cucumber GI ~15, tomato GI ~15, onion GI ~10) as reported in the University of Sydney GI Database and Atkinson et al. (2021). The high water and dietary fibre content, combined with the absence of starchy ingredients, supports a low GI classification. Glycemic load per 150 g serving calculated as: GL = (GI × available carbohydrate per serving) / 100 = (15 × 8.25) / 100 ≈ 1.2.

hypertension.guidance: Kachumber Salad contains approximately 200 mg sodium per 100 g due to added salt; individuals with hypertension should prepare it with minimal or no added salt to leverage its naturally high potassium content (180 mg/100 g), which supports blood pressure regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Low glycemic index and dietary fibre content
type2_diabetes.guidance: With a glycemic index of approximately 15 and 1.5 g dietary fibre per 100 g, Kachumber Salad produces a minimal postprandial glucose response and is suitable as a regular side dish for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Low saturated fat, zero cholesterol, and antioxidant micronutrients
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Kachumber Salad is cholesterol-free, very low in saturated fat (0.03 g/100 g), and provides vitamin C (15 mg/100 g) and vitamin A (30 mcg RAE/100 g), supporting endothelial health and making it a cardioprotective side dish when prepared without excess salt.

rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: The vitamin C and antioxidant content of Kachumber Salad may support anti-inflammatory pathways; individuals with rheumatoid arthritis who are sensitive to nightshades should note the tomato content, though evidence for nightshade avoidance in RA is not conclusive.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Kachumber Salad is a traditional Indian raw vegetable salad made from finely chopped cucumber, tomato, and onion, dressed with fresh lemon juice and optionally seasoned with green chili, chaat masala, and fresh coriander leaves. It is a no-cook, minimally processed dish that requires no heat preparation. The texture is crisp and refreshing, with a bright, tangy flavour profile from the lemon juice balanced by the mild heat of chili and the aromatic earthiness of chaat masala. Kachumber is served pan-Indian as a side dish accompanying heavier meals such as dal, biryani, or grilled meats, and is also consumed as a light snack or appetizer. Nutritionally, it is very low in calories (approximately 25 kcal per 100 g), low in fat (0.2 g/100 g), and provides modest dietary fibre (1.5 g/100 g), vitamin C (15 mg/100 g), and potassium (180 mg/100 g). Its glycemic index is estimated at approximately 15 (low), making it suitable for individuals managing Type 2 Diabetes, hypertension, and cardiovascular disease. The dish is naturally vegan, gluten-free, and dairy-free. Sodium content (approximately 200 mg/100 g) reflects typical light salting and should be reduced for sodium-restricted diets.

confidence_reason: Kachumber Salad is a composite raw vegetable dish without a single dedicated entry in ICMR-NIN published tables. Nutrient values are adapted from ICMR-NIN component data for cucumber, tomato, onion, and lemon juice. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database using individual vegetable GI values. Confidence is medium rather than high because no single approved-source entry covers the exact composite dish.
notes: 1. Cholesterol: The input record reported 26 mg/100 g cholesterol for this plant-based raw salad. This is biologically implausible as Kachumber Salad contains no animal-derived ingredients. The value has been corrected to 0.0 mg/100 g, consistent with ICMR-NIN data for the constituent vegetables. The original input meta attributed this value to 'hpb' source; however, no HPB Singapore entry for Kachumber Salad was located, and the value is inconsistent with the food's composition. 2. Vitamin B12: Set to 0.0 mcg — confirmed zero as this is a fully plant-based dish with no animal-derived ingredients; vitamin B12 is naturally absent. Vitamin D: Set to 0.0 mcg — naturally zero for a raw vegetable salad with no fortified or animal-derived ingredients. Trans fat: Set to 0.0 g — naturally zero for a raw vegetable dish. 3. water_g set to -1: detailed moisture data for the composite Kachumber Salad preparation is not separately tabulated in ICMR-NIN published tables as of 2024; individual vegetable moisture values exist but a blended composite figure for this specific preparation is not available in any approved source consulted. 4. Portions: The input record contained three identical duplicate portion entries (all '1 serving (150g)', 150 g, is_default: true). Deduplicated to a single canonical serving of 150 g. 5. Sodium of 200 mg/100 g reflects light salting typical of home preparation; unsalted preparation would yield approximately 10–20 mg/100 g sodium from the vegetables alone. 6. Ontology: FoodOn URI FOODON_03301710 and FoodEx2 code A00GJ are approximate matches; no exact codes exist for Kachumber Salad in either system as of 2024. 7. Multilingual: Kachumber Salad is an Indian dish; mandatory language per Project Veritas rules is Hindi (satisfied by कचुम्बर). Chinese, Malay, Tamil are not mandatory for Indian cuisine and are not applicable here.
